mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate below 0.5% signals an ultra-loose monetary regime that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset price multiples. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate remains in the zero-bound zone.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate above 9% signals labor market slack and weak aggregate demand, which compresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism is triggered when UNRATE crosses above 9% and remains active until it falls below 8%.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 9.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months
regime_hint: elevated unemployment / weak labor market regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite UNRATE > 9%, or correlation between UNRATE and forward returns becomes insignificant (p > 0.10) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) declining month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand and foreshadows earnings downgrades. This threshold-triggered mechanism activates when RSAFS growth turns negative and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth turns negative (MoM change < 0)
evidence_refs: fact:current RSAFS=339993M suggests recent demand level
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances following negative RSAFS MoM prints, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal supply-side capacity utilization. When both INDPRO and PAYEMS grow above their 3-month trend simultaneously, the mechanism indicates tight supply conditions that support corporate pricing power and margins, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=97.07 and PAYEMS=132151 provide current capacity signals
regime_hint: tight supply / strong capacity utilization regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite joint INDPRO and PAYEMS strength, or interaction effect (measured via regression coefficient on INDPRO×PAYEMS term) becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) below 400K units signals structural demand weakness in the real estate sector and foreshadows reduced construction employment and consumer wealth effects. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days when housing activity falls below this threshold.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 400K units and remains below this level for 2+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST=458K is near but above critical threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of periods when HOUST < 400K, or the predictive power of HOUST deteriorates (Rank IC < 0.02) in recent 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 3% combined with FEDFUNDS below 2% signals negative real rates that incentivize equity risk-taking and asset price appreciation. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when real monetary conditions are accommodative.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=212.67 and FEDFUNDS=0.18% indicate negative real rates
regime_hint: negative real rates / financial repression regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite negative real rates, or the joint interaction term (CPIAUCSL × FEDFUNDS) becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerating below 50K per month signals labor market momentum loss and foreshadows earnings downgrades. This threshold-triggered mechanism activates when monthly payroll growth turns weak and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices recession risk.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 50K persons
evidence_refs: fact:current PAYEMS=132151K provides employment level reference
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances following weak PAYEMS prints (MoM < 50K), or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth below 1% annualized signals late-cycle stagnation and earnings recession risk. This regime-conditional mechanism is active only in late-cycle expansions and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days when growth momentum deteriorates below this threshold.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 1.0% in consecutive quarters
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of late-cycle periods when GDPC1 growth < 1%, or the predictive relationship between GDPC1 growth and forward returns becomes insignificant (p > 0.10) in walk-forward tests.
